summary:
# Master Summary: Acme Corp / BuildrCo Pvt Ltd — Customer Onboarding Platform Engagement

---

## 1. Overall Arrangement

Acme Corp has engaged BuildrCo Pvt Ltd to design, build, and deploy a customer onboarding platform to replace Acme's existing manual process. The engagement is structured across three interlocking documents: a **Master Services Agreement (MSA)** establishing the overarching commercial and legal framework, a **Statement of Work (SOW-01)** defining the specific scope, deliverables, and timeline, and a **Change Order (CO-01)** amending SOW-01 to add CRM integration and accelerate go-live. Together, these documents constitute a single integrated contractual arrangement with a total stated value of **INR 56,00,000**, subject to CO-01 being executed (see critical caveat below).

---

## 2. Parties and Their Relationship

| Party | Role | Location |
|---|---|---|
| Acme Corp | Client | Bengaluru |
| BuildrCo Pvt Ltd | Vendor | Bengaluru |

The relationship is a **client-vendor services engagement**, not a partnership or joint venture. BuildrCo performs and delivers; Acme pays, provides inputs, and accepts deliverables. The MSA governs all SOWs that may be issued under it, meaning the framework is designed to accommodate future work beyond SOW-01. The designated Vendor contact is **Ms. R. Iyer (Project Manager)**.

---

## 3. Scope of Work

The core deliverables under SOW-01, as amended by CO-01, are:

- **Onboarding web application** — registration workflow, document upload module, and administrative console
- **Data migration** — migration of existing customer records with cleansing and validation
- **Performance optimization** prior to go-live
- **Salesforce CRM integration** (added by CO-01) — real-time sync, field mapping, and error handling; accepted upon successful integration test demonstrating record sync across all mapped fields
- **30-day post-launch hypercare support** — defect triage and resolution

---

## 4. Timeline and Key Milestones

| Event | Original Date (SOW-01) | Amended Date (CO-01) |
|---|---|---|
| Project Start | 1 August 2025 | Unchanged |
| Client provides legacy system access & test data | 20 August 2025 | Unchanged |
| Client confirms final data schema | 25 August 2025 | Unchanged |
| Platform Build Complete | 15 September 2025 | Unchanged |
| **Target Go-Live** | **5 October 2025** | **25 September 2025** |
| UAT Sign-Off | 30 September 2025 | Unchanged |
| Data Migration Complete | 15 October 2025 | Unchanged |
| Hypercare Support Ends | 30 days post-launch | 30 days post-launch |

**Critical interdependency and tension:** CO-01 accelerates go-live to **25 September 2025**, which is **five days before** the UAT Sign-Off date of **30 September 2025** that remains unchanged from SOW-01. This creates a direct logical conflict — the platform cannot go live before UAT is formally signed off without either waiving the UAT gate or treating go-live and UAT sign-off as concurrent events. This inconsistency is unresolved on the face of the documents and represents a significant execution risk.

---

## 5. Commercial Terms

### Fee Structure

| Milestone | Trigger | Amount (INR) |
|---|---|---|
| Milestone 1 | Signing of MSA/SOW | 16,00,000 |
| Milestone 2 | Platform Build Complete | 16,00

citations.text_span: 01_master_services_agreement MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT This Master Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into on 1 August 2025 (the "Effective Date")
